item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wow! It has made the achievement to be an SCP that the foundation wants to kill without making it look like SCP-682! That is really good with great detail and really felt as if it was a real document of the foundation. The only thing I didn't like was the interview, it doe's not added a lot.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Kill over 10 million people and destroy hundreds of square miles of land or let loose a flesh monster and its vampire drones on a random city with a thousand deaths every year?

The second one sounds like the least destructive option.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Jesus, the detail in this SCP article is just astonishing.

I really hope someone makes a tale on this SCP or an exploration log.

Overall, amazing SCP. That's a +1 from me!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Further deviations from baseline homo sapiens include especially flat, upturned noses, and funnel-shaped ears; both considered related to their dependency on olfactory and auditory perception.

Noserfartu? Is that you?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I was gone for a year, saw that series 4 had started, decided to pick back up in three and what a piece to come back to.

+ show block
– hide block

Even thou the more I read the more I wondered why the foundation has not contained the anomaly better.
If there is a core, a point of origin, than it should be easy to trim the anomaly down to a reasonable size.

The letter tries to explain it, but for me the containment would most likely not have costed this many casualties.

We know its energy source, therefore we know from where it draws its energy.
Since there is no obvious brain to the whole thing my first thought, pumping it full with enough amnesic agent that would it forgets how to move, would most likely not work.

My next idea would include radioactivity.
The organism relies on precise mutations. So blocking those mutations would make cretin poisoned regions unattractive to continue growth in said region.

If the anomaly would try to use earthquakes to destroy the region it would lose large amounts of energy.
Why would it do that? The anomaly would most likely give up on that region.

Than there is the problem with its sentience and reasoning.
From what I could gather the object actually dose not display higher intelligence.
If the anomaly had millennia to grow and has still not escaped its current location it either lags ambition or it is unable to for some reason.
What ever that reason might be it could help containment efforts.

I know my review style is nit-picky, but some things grind my gears and its the "what if"s that bring these thinks alive for me.

It is good.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: +1 for the note from the O5 council, especially the end.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Just noticed your message now. I must have mixed the forest up with another or assumed it was dense due to the number of disappearances associated with it. I like to be accurate where it matters and will look into correcting this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I found out today that Hoia Forest is only about 1 square mile in area and directly adjacent to the city of Cluj-Napoca, which means it is frequently used for recreational activities and is bisected by a main road. The 10km perimeter specified in the containment procedures would therefore extend for miles outside the forest and require the whole of Cluj-Napoca city (with a population of ~325,000) to be evicted.

I like this article, and there's nothing wrong with setting the article in a parallel or future setting where Cluj-Napoca is abandoned, but as it is the article treats the Hoia forest like it's a vast, dense forest when it's really a pretty small forest on the doorstep of a substantial city, which is both factually inaccurate and breaks suspension of disbelief that the anomaly could be reasonably covered up using the current procedures.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Is 2191-3 supposed to be Lovataar?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ooh. I like this, really feels like a Series 1 idea with modern execution.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Was this by any chance inspired by La-Mulana? I mean, two supermassive Eldrich horrors that live deep underground in temples and are recognized as mother goddesses seems like more than coincidence. Not to mention their followers refer to both of them simply as "Mother" and the creature from La-Mulana also uses giant tentacles to carve caverns and feed

I love the idea regardless and this article was very well done.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is very, very nice.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You never cease to amaze me, Meta. Brilliant work.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: "> 100nm" includes far ultraviolet all the way through the visible spectrum and infrared. It probably doesn't include microwave and radio because we don't tend to call that portion of the spectrum "light". I think the intent is that any light (from 100nm to 1mm) regardless of intensity causes this reaction (although really there probably ought to be a minimum).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Their regressed eyes are covered by a layer of skin, rendering them mostly blind but still able to react to light (universally displaying aversion to wavelengths > 100 nm).

100 nm is ultraviolet light. They might be averse to letting ultraviolet fall on their skin, due to having no pigmentation, but the above implies that they can see ultraviolet and don't like it. It also implies they can see infrared, microwave and so on. Maybe you want to use lux as a measurement of brightness?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, I think if you look them up on the wiki, you can get a much clearer definition of the two than you'd ever get from me. As far as I know, Keters can and usually are more dangerous than Euclids, and tend to be much more difficult to contain.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you for the answer Kobold, I now have a better understanding of why it has this classification.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Most likely because it is

A). More difficult to contain than a Euclid

B). Much more threatening than a Euclid

or

C). Both

I say C.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I 've read your article from begin to end, but I don't understand why the object class "Keter" is.
Formerly, it was Euclid and I should clasificate this SCP as an Euclid object. Maybe my english is still to weak. Can someone explain why the object class isn't euclid?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So, I saw you in the forums pitching a "Vampire factory," and my only thoughts were, "Metaphysician, that's dumb. You're doing a dumb."

I was very, very wrong, and I apologize. I am the dumb. Have a +1 in reconciliation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
For where the name "Dracula Factory" came from.

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=gfIvKC9MKQ4

Sadly, I can't find the clip where he originally declares it a "Dracula Factory":p

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks! That means a lot!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love every single part of this, from the detailed description of how the 'vampires' function, to the way you made them function, to how it was tied into vampire mythology, to how you made them connected to this eldritch worm monster thing, the connection to Adytum and whatnot, and the O5 letter. The articles surrounding this particular mythology are some of the better things to come out of the site in recent times, IMO.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh! I should really read the titles instead of just jumping in from Most Recently Created. That might've put a different spin on things.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm unfamiliar with that word. You don't maybe mean "non-anomalous", do you?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Wow, that felt similar to the Cancerverse in Marvel. Nonetheless, I am only excited to see what is in store for you.

It's fascinating how the many pieces come together to unfold an universe greater than itself.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I've considered writing a tale from Ion's point of view. A man who sold out our world. A weak slave who started a rebellion in a part of the Daeva empire near the Ural mountains and… vanished, its plans somehow delayed by the CotBG and other actors, but not without triggering the Bronze Age Collapse. ALthough, as hinted with a lot of my SCP. They were not destroyed and, still have loyal agents in the world secretly building bases of power. Some cults in strange, hidden parts of the world - some in places of power, attracting politicians and business leaders; people willing to sacrifice others in order to get ahead.

In my mind, as I tried to hint with other SCP, Ion and "Adytum" (originally a city state ruled by the Daeva, now much more) was cast into another iteration of our world. And consumed it. He presents himself as almost like the pope of these cults but I think of him more as a middle-man, making terrible bargains with entities outside our reality - chaotic, Lovecraftian sort of horrors.

And now, with its various cults waiting, eagerly for their sorcerer-king, their perverted reality is bleeding back into ours. I don't think of Ion as human anymore. Or at least, its built a godlike presence around itself, carved from flesh and shaped by anomalous relics, to hide that formerly mortal core.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
As much as normal people shouldn't root for the villains, I would like to see more of the mysterious Sarkic cults and their Grand Karcist Ion.

What on earth makes you think that anyone on this entire site is normal?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Adding on to the other stuff I had said in chat and on forum, I am quite curious about the Sarkic cults/Flesh weaponising flesh and blood for their purpose. Would there be a possible connection with Bigfoot (who do utilise biopunk technology)?

As much as normal people shouldn't root for the villains, I would like to see more of the mysterious Sarkic cults and their Grand Karcist Ion.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
…and this is officially one of my favorite skips ever.
+ $\infty$ (but its only counting the first time I hit the button, so that will have to do)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I admit, the vampire angle was there from the start. Thus, "Dracula Factory";p I wanted to approach a standard spooky creature from a new angle, especially since this was meant for Halloween.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Aside from the confusion regarding what 2A, 2B and 2C are and how they fit together, it is very well written and VERY creepy! Well done!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think this is very well written. I liked the O5 statement and the reference to Adytum, although I think that was almost secondary, or out-of-context: a nugget of exposition slid rather than worked in.

For me, though, my interest kind of flagged when I realized that these were reworked vampires. There was plenty of additional detail after that, but the big critter wasn't strong enough to bring me back in. Figuratively, anyway. No vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Liking this a lot; I'd love to see an exploration log.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks! That is one thing I worried about, at the same time, I wanted to detail everything to give a feel of a strange, symbiotic hive of things that once shared our humanity;p

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The quality of the writing and horror here astounds me. Many times I recoiled or went "Ewww", and it harkens back to the old-school scary of Series 1 perfectly fine with the build-up and execution of your narrative akin to Series 2 & 3. My only complaint is that at times the description felt a bit overly long to read through, and it felt like a chore.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This was actually meant for Halloween but I never finished and put it aside for months. Finally think it is worth posting. I hope people enjoy it.
